calibration_json.description: Raw camera calibration JSON. Expected fields are documented in app/worker/zones.py
calibration_json.title: Calibration Json

EnqueueEmbeddingRequest.description:
Request payload for enqueuing Phase 2 embedding jobs.

Why this exists:
- Parsing and embedding are separate background steps.
- If embeddings were disabled or failed earlier, we need a safe manual
  "retry embeddings" entrypoint to unblock Phase 3 screening runs.
EnqueueEmbeddingRequest.title: EnqueueEmbeddingRequest

EssProfilePatchIn.description:
ESS profile patch: allow safe fields only.

We deliberately allow extra keys so we can return a stable 400 AppError
instead of a generic 422 validation error.
EssProfilePatchIn.title: EssProfilePatchIn

MobileAccountListOut.description:
Admin response for listing mobile account mappings for a store.

This lets the dashboard show, per employee:
- whether mobile access is provisioned
- the Firebase UID (for debugging/support)
- active/revoked state
MobileAccountListOut.title: MobileAccountListOut

MobileProvisionIn.description:
Admin request to provision mobile access for an employee.

Notes:
- Email/password is the MVP. If `temp_password` is omitted, the backend generates one.
- `phone_number` support is included for future Firebase Phone Auth flows.

temp_password.description: Optional temporary password for email flow; if omitted, backend generates one.
temp_password.title: Temp Password

MobileProvisionIn.title: MobileProvisionIn

PayrollPeriodCreateIn.description: Create a period for a calendar (inclusive end_date).
PayrollPeriodCreateIn.title: PayrollPeriodCreateIn

ProfileChangeCreateIn.description: ESS submission payload for a new profile change request.
ProfileChangeCreateIn.title: ProfileChangeCreateIn

PunchSettingsUpdateIn.description: Update request for punch settings (partial update via PUT in v1).
PunchSettingsUpdateIn.title: PunchSettingsUpdateIn

PunchSubmitIn.description:
Punch-in/out submission payload.

Notes:
- `idempotency_key` enables client retries without duplicating punches.
- `meta` is a future-proof JSON container for non-sensitive client context
  (e.g., device metadata). We intentionally do not enforce a schema in v1.
PunchSubmitIn.title: PunchSubmitIn

category.description: Optional category (only used if we create the skill).
category.title: Category

name.description: Optional human-friendly name (only used if we create the skill).
name.title: Name

RosterOverrideUpsertIn.description: Upsert a per-employee per-day roster override.
RosterOverrideUpsertIn.title: RosterOverrideUpsertIn

ScreeningRunCreateRequest.description:
Optional overrides for run configuration.

If omitted, the defaults from Settings are used.
ScreeningRunCreateRequest.title: ScreeningRunCreateRequest

ShiftAssignmentCreateIn.description: Assign a shift template to an employee (effective dated).
ShiftAssignmentCreateIn.title: ShiftAssignmentCreateIn

get.description:
Tenant-scoped payable summaries list for HR/admin reporting.

v1:
- This endpoint reads precomputed rows from attendance.payable_day_summaries.
- For recompute, use POST /attendance/payable/recompute.

schema.title: Response Admin Payable Days Api V1 Attendance Admin Payable Days Get
Response Admin List Corrections Api V1 Attendance Corrections Get
Response Me Corrections Api V1 Attendance Me Corrections Get
Response Me Submit Correction Api V1 Attendance Me Corrections Post
Response Me Cancel Correction Api V1 Attendance Me Corrections  Correction Id  Cancel Post
Response Me Days Api V1 Attendance Me Days Get

get.description:
Return payroll-ready payable summaries for the actor (ESS).

v1 behavior:
- This endpoint computes summaries on demand for small ranges and upserts
  them into attendance.payable_day_summaries.
- Worked minutes are override-aware (leave/corrections precedence).

schema.title: Response Me Payable Days Api V1 Attendance Me Payable Days Get

post.description:
Punch in (Zoho-style toggle).

v1:
- Source is hardcoded to MANUAL_WEB for ESS routes.
- Clients may supply an idempotency_key for safe retries.

schema.title: Response Me Punch In Api V1 Attendance Me Punch In Post

post.description:
Punch out (closes the OPEN session).

See `/me/punch-in` for source/idempotency semantics.

schema.title: Response Me Punch Out Api V1 Attendance Me Punch Out Post

get.description: Return current punch state + effective minutes for today (branch-local).

schema.title: Response Me Punch State Api V1 Attendance Me Punch State Get

post.description:
Recompute payable summaries for a range.

Safety:
- v1 requires either branch_id or employee_ids to be provided to avoid
  recomputing an entire tenant accidentally.

schema.title: Response Recompute Payable Days Api V1 Attendance Payable Recompute Post

get.description:
Read (or lazily initialize) punch settings for a branch.

Security:
- Query parameters do not automatically participate in scope resolution.
  We harden by requiring the active scope branch_id matches the requested branch.

schema.title: Response Get Punch Settings Api V1 Attendance Punch Settings Get

put.description:
Create or update punch settings for a branch.

v1 treats this as "upsert" (create if missing).

schema.title: Response Put Punch Settings Api V1 Attendance Punch Settings Put

get.description:
Return payable summaries for the manager's team (MSS).

v1:
- Uses `hr_core.repo_mss.list_team_directory` to determine the subtree.
- Computes summaries on demand for small ranges and returns a flat list of rows.

schema.title: Response Team Payable Days Api V1 Attendance Team Payable Days Get
Response Login Api V1 Auth Login Post
Response Logout Api V1 Auth Logout Post
Response Me Api V1 Auth Me Get
Response Refresh Api V1 Auth Refresh Post
Response Bootstrap Api V1 Bootstrap Post

patch.description: Move an application to another stage (drag/drop) or update stage by name.

schema.title: Response Update Application Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Applications  Application Id  Patch

Response List Notes Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Applications  Application Id  Notes Get
Response Create Note Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Applications  Application Id  Notes Post
Response Reject Application Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Applications  Application Id  Reject Post

get.description:
Secure download:
- artifact.path is stored as a relative path
- we refuse any path that resolves outside settings.data_dir

schema.title: Response List Attendance Daily Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Attendance Daily Get
Response List Cameras Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Cameras Get

post.description:
Register a training image for an employee.

Behavior:
- Detect best face in the uploaded image
- Crop the face
- Save to <FACE_DIR>/<tenant_id>/<branch_id>/<employee_id>/<timestamp>.jpg
- Clear prototypes (they will rebuild on next recognition)

schema.title: Response Register Face Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Employees  Employee Id  Faces Register Post

delete.description: Delete one training image for an employee.

schema.title: Response Delete Training Image Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Employees  Employee Id  Faces  Filename  Delete

get.description:
Secure view/download endpoint for per-event snapshots (entry/exit frames).

This is useful for the UI to show a thumbnail/preview while keeping disk paths opaque.
List training images per employee for a branch.

schema.title: Response List Faces Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Faces Get

post.description: Clear in-memory prototypes for a store.

schema.title: Response Clear Prototypes Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Faces Clear Post

post.description: Trigger an async prototype rebuild for a store.

schema.title: Response Rebuild Prototypes Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Faces Rebuild Post

post.description: Recognize a face image against current branch prototypes.

schema.title: Response Recognize Face Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Faces Recognize Post
Response Upload Import Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Imports Post
Response Publish Import Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Imports  Dataset Id  Publish Post
Response Get Job Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Get
Response List Artifacts Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Artifacts Get
Response List Attendance Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Attendance Get
Response Cancel Job Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Cancel Post
Response List Events Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Events Get
Response List Metrics Hourly Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Metrics Hourly Get
Response Recompute Job Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Recompute Post
Response Retry Job Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Jobs  Job Id  Retry Post

get.description:
List the current mobile access mappings for a branch.

Frontend use-case:
- Show a "Mobile" column on the Employees page.
- Decide whether to show "Provision" vs "Revoke/Resync".

This endpoint is permission-gated via RBAC (`mobile:accounts:read`).

schema.title: Response List Branch Mobile Accounts Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Mobile Accounts Get
Response Leaderboard Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Months  Month Key  Leaderboard Get
Response Sync Mobile Month Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Months  Month Key  Sync Mobile Post
Response List Openings Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings Get
Response Create Opening Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings Post
Response Get Opening Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Get
Response Update Opening Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Patch

get.description:
List applications for an opening (Kanban-friendly).

Filters:
- stage_id: show one column
- status: ACTIVE|REJECTED|HIRED

schema.title: Response List Applications Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Applications Get

post.description:
Manually create an application from a resume.

This is useful when:
- a resume was uploaded but not part of a ScreeningRun, or
- you want to add an applicant directly to the pipeline.

schema.title: Response Create Application Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Applications Post

get.description:
List pipeline stages for an opening (ordered by sort_order).

This endpoint also lazily creates default stages if none exist. This avoids
footguns when working with openings created before Phase 5 was installed.

schema.title: Response List Pipeline Stages Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Pipeline Stages Get

patch.description:
Update a pipeline stage (rename, reorder, mark terminal).

This is optional for MVP but is useful for quickly iterating on pipelines.

schema.title: Response Update Pipeline Stage Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Pipeline Stages  Stage Id  Patch
Response List Resumes Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Resumes Get
Response Get Batch Status Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Resumes Batch  Batch Id  Status Get

post.description:
Phase 2: enqueue embeddings for already-parsed resumes in an opening.

Notes:
- This endpoint never embeds inline; it only enqueues RQ jobs.
- Resumes must be PARSED (clean text artifacts exist on disk).
- We skip resumes that are already EMBEDDED unless `force=true`.

schema.title: Response Enqueue Opening Embeddings Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Resumes Embed Post

get.description:
Phase 2 debug endpoint: counts of parsing + embedding state for one opening.

This is intentionally simple and UI-friendly so you can quickly see whether
the background pipeline is keeping up.

schema.title: Response Get Opening Index Status Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Resumes Index Status Get

post.description:
Upload 1..N resumes for an opening.

Flow:
1) Create hr_resumes rows (status=UPLOADED) and store files to disk.
2) Commit rows so worker can see them.
3) Enqueue RQ jobs (one per resume) to parse using Unstructured.

schema.title: Response Upload Resumes Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Resumes Upload Post

post.description:
Create and enqueue a ScreeningRun for an opening.

This mirrors the "create job + enqueue" pattern used by the CCTV pipeline,
but uses separate tables/endpoints for HR.

schema.title: Response Create Screening Run Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Screening Runs Post

post.description:
Phase 2 debug endpoint (MVP retrieval):
- Embed the query using the configured HuggingFace model.
- Search embedded "full" resume views within the opening using pgvector cosine distance.

This is *not* a full ATS ranking pipeline; it's only for debugging retrieval quality.

schema.title: Response Search Resumes Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Openings  Opening Id  Search Post

get.description:
Return the parsed.json artifact for debugging.

If the resume is not parsed yet, we return 409 with status info.

schema.title: Response Get Parsed Resume Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Resumes  Resume Id  Parsed Get

get.description:
Phase 2 debug endpoint: list which views are stored for a resume and whether
they have embeddings.

schema.title: Response List Resume Views Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Resumes  Resume Id  Views Get
Response Get Screening Run Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Get

post.description:
Bulk-create applications from a ScreeningRun.

Supported modes:
- Explicit resume_ids list
- top_n: take the top N ranked results from the run (requires run.status == DONE)

schema.title: Response Create Applications From Run Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Applications Post

post.description:
Cancel a ScreeningRun.

Cancellation is best-effort:
- If the job is already running, the worker will check DB state and stop at the next checkpoint.
- If the job is queued, it may still start briefly and exit immediately.

schema.title: Response Cancel Screening Run Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Cancel Post

post.description:
Manually enqueue explanation generation for the top N results of a run.

This endpoint NEVER blocks on LLM calls; it only enqueues an RQ job.

schema.title: Response Enqueue Run Explanations Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Explanations Post
Response List Screening Results Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Results Get

get.description:
Fetch the stored explanation for one result row.

Returns 404 if explanations haven't been generated yet for this candidate.

schema.title: Response Get Screening Explanation Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Results  Resume Id  Explanation Get

post.description:
Enqueue explanation generation for a single candidate.

Body:
  { "force": true }  # overwrite existing explanation if present

schema.title: Response Enqueue Single Explanation Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Results  Resume Id  Explanation Recompute Post

post.description:
Retry a ScreeningRun by creating a NEW run with the same config.

We do this to preserve audit history (the old run remains immutable).

schema.title: Response Retry Screening Run Api V1 Branches  Branch Id  Screening Runs  Run Id  Retry Post
